title: Sediment loading at the southern Chilean trench and its tectonic implications
abstract: Seismic and bathymetric data acquired off south-central Chile were used to estimate representative flexural rigidities and study the downward deflection of the oceanic Nazca plate due to sediment loading. Flexural modeling shows that efficient sediment subduction preferentially occurs in weak oceanic lithosphere, whereas wide accretionary prisms form in rigid oceanic lithosphere. Results suggest a general increase of fluid pressure inside the continental wedge north of 33°S.

location.description: Southern Chile Trench from 33°S to 46°S; flexural modeling using seismic and bathymetric constraints

seismic_lines.name: Compilation of MCS profiles — south-central Chile trench (33°–46°S)

data.name: Compiled MCS Profiles — South-Central Chile Trench 33°–46°S
data.description: Compilation of seismic reflection profiles from SPOC, TIPTEQ and other cruises used to constrain trench geometry and sediment loading for flexural modeling between 33°–46°S. [SPOC cruise (SO161, 2001) datasets searchable on PANGAEA]

data.name: Swath Bathymetry — South-Central Chile
data.description: Multibeam bathymetry used to define trench geometry and sediment distribution for flexural calculations. [SPOC cruise (SO161, 2001) datasets searchable on PANGAEA]

_data_note: Modeling paper using published seismic and bathymetric data from SPOC/TIPTEQ and other cruises. No new data acquisition.

processing.summary: Published seismic reflection profiles and bathymetric data from south-central Chile (33°–46°S) were compiled and used to constrain trench geometry for flexural rigidity calculations. Elastic plate modeling shows that plate rigidity controls whether sediment is subducted (weak lithosphere) or accreted (rigid lithosphere). The north-south gradient in fluid pressure implied by the results links to the erosive-to-accretionary transition at ~33°S.
workflow: 1. Compilation of published MCS and bathymetric data
4. Comparison of modeled and observed deflections
processing.notes: Modeling paper; seismic data compiled from SPOC, TIPTEQ, and earlier cruises

analysis_notes: PDF validated 2026-04-05. Corrected: authors list (added Jorge Jara and Wilhelm Weinrebe, both missing); DOI corrected from 10.1016/j.jog.2013.03.005→10.1016/j.jog.2013.02.009 (confirmed from paper footer and Appendix B); URL updated. This is a pure modeling study — no new seismic data acquisition, compiled SPOC/TIPTEQ data. No data availability statement found.
